root_text.ud4.9:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.ud4.9:1.3: Atha kho āyasmato upasenassa vaṅgantaputtassa rahogatassa paṭisallīnassa evaṁ cetaso parivitakko udapādi:
root_text.ud4.9:1.5: satthā ca me bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho;
root_text.ud4.9:1.6: svākkhāte camhi dhammavinaye agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajito;
root_text.ud4.9:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā āyasmato upasenassa vaṅgantaputtassa cetasā cetoparivitakkamaññāya tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud4.9:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.ud4.9:1.3: Then as Venerable Upasena son of Vaṅgantā was in private retreat this thought came to his mind:
translation_text.ud4.9:1.5: My Teacher is the Blessed One, perfected, a fully awakened Buddha.
translation_text.ud4.9:1.6: Well explained is the teaching and training in which I have gone forth from the lay life to homelessness.
translation_text.ud4.9:1.7: My spiritual companions are ethical and of good character.
translation_text.ud4.9:1.9: My mind is unified and serene.
translation_text.ud4.9:1.10: I am a perfected one who has ended the defilements.
translation_text.ud4.9:1.11: I am of great might and power.
translation_text.ud4.9:1.12: My life has been good, and my death will be good.”
translation_text.ud4.9:2.1: Then, knowing that Venerable Upasena’s train of thought, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud4.9:3.1: “One who has no guilt in life,
translation_text.ud4.9:3.3: that attentive one who has seen the truth,
translation_text.ud4.9:4.1: For the mendicant with peaceful mind,
translation_text.ud4.9:4.2: who has cut off craving for continued existence,
translation_text.ud4.9:4.4: there’ll be no more future lives for them.”
